vocabulary.word: pitch / track / court / course / ring / rink

phrasal_verbs.meaning: change the date/time of an event so it happens earlier
defeat and remove from a competition; make unconscious
start (after planning to do sth for a long time)
stop being involved in an activity
do; do sth you should not do
start (a hobby, sport, etc); fill an amount of space/time

A.title: Topic vocabulary in contrast
A.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

questions.sentence: We used to go skating at the ice ...... every Saturday.
Keith had never seen such a large golf ...... until he went to Scotland.
We all met at the basketball ...... at half past three.
It's called a boxing ...... but it's actually square!
For the 800 metres race, you have to run round the ...... twice.
The football match had to be called off because the ...... was flooded.
Our team ...... until half time, but in the second half the other team ...... three goals, and so they ...... us. But it was a great match!
Adrian got a new fishing ...... for his birthday.
Can I borrow your tennis ......?
Why are hockey ...... such a strange shape?
I'd spend hours putting linseed oil on my cricket ...... to keep the wood strong.

B.title: Circle the correct word
B.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

questions.sentence: The **umpire / referee** blew the whistle and the most important football match I've ever played began.
We used to play rugby in the winter term, football in the spring term, and we'd do **athletics / sport** and swimming in the summer term.
Do you fancy a **game / play** of cards?
I only do magic tricks for fun. I've never thought of becoming an **amateur / a professional** magician.
The play was so boring, we walked out during half **time / the interval**.
Coventry City **equalled / drew** 3-3 with Sunderland in the match last Saturday.
**Spectators / Viewers** who watched last week's programme will remember we were looking at the history of baseball.
We got through to the **final / finale**, but then lost to Cirencester.
Most people prefer films which have a happy **end / ending**.
Would all **opponents / competitors** please make their way to the starting line?

C.title: Phrasal verbs (transformation)
C.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
I don't know how you can stand getting up so early to go to the pool. **put**
I don't know how you can ...... up so early to go to the pool.

I've finally started sorting out my postcard collection. **round**
I've finally ...... sorting out my postcard collection.

What did you do at the weekend? **get**
What did you ...... at the weekend?

I'm not so keen on skiing now I've discovered snowboarding. **gone**
I've ...... since I discovered snowboarding.

Why do you continue to have riding lessons if you can't afford them? **on**
Why do you ...... riding lessons if you can't afford them?

We can't delay the match any longer. **put**
We can't ...... any longer.

D.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
D.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Tony never used to want to join ...... with the other kids in the playground.
Look ...... ! There's a car coming!
Simone's ...... to wearing a helmet whenever she goes cycling.
I was thinking of taking ...... scuba diving until I found out how expensive the equipment is.
They were knocked ...... in the semi-final.
Maybe we should bring the meeting ...... to this Tuesday instead of having it in two weeks' time.
Becca had to pull ...... of the race when she sprained her ankle.
Melissa doesn't ...... in for adventure sports.
Why don't you ...... a go? It's not difficult!
Carl wasn't very good at mountain climbing as he's afraid of .......
There's little ...... of our getting into the final.
Rachel is mad ...... the Eurovision Song Contest.
Just ...... your best - that's all anyone can ask of you.
It's ...... time you learned to swim.
Grandma ...... a lot of pleasure from gardening.
...... your time - don't rush.
Each player takes it ...... turn to roll the dice.
Time ...... so quickly when you're doing something enjoyable.
Our new coach is popular ...... the whole team.
She'd ...... hours lying on her bed, reading.
He'd been planning to leave the team the ...... time, and hadn't told anyone.
I'm not siding ...... her because she's my sister, but because she's right.
I'm not very fond **D** of playing in goal.
She's interested **A** in playing for the school team.
questions.answer: A – in playing for the school team.

questions.sentence: You should concentrate **G** on getting fit.
I used to find it difficult **B** to find enough time to have any hobbies.
questions.answer: B – to find enough time to have any hobbies.

questions.sentence: Let's listen **F** to what the coach thinks first.
questions.answer: F – to what the coach thinks first.

questions.sentence: We stopped **C** to try to find the golf balls we'd lost.
questions.answer: C – to try to find the golf balls we'd lost.

questions.sentence: The bad weather stopped us **E** from finishing the game.

G.title: Cloze (Word patterns)
G.instructions: Water has damaged part of this text. Read it and decide what you think each of the original words was. Write the words in the blank spaces.

questions.sentence: I used to wear glasses when I was at school, and so I ...... sport very difficult.
I wanted to be involved ...... school sports competitions.
and I loved the idea of teams competing ...... each other,
but being on the rugby team, for example, ...... having to take off my glasses,
and ...... meant I couldn't see!
And it's difficult ...... catch a ball when you can't see it!
So, I wasn't very good and the captains always ...... sure that I wasn't on their team.
This meant I ...... free to do other activities, like being on the debating team,
and actually I preferred to ...... things I was good at doing than have everyone laugh at me.
than have everyone laugh at me.

H.title: Word formation (sentences)
H.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

questions.sentence: I need to buy a new pair of ...... (TRAIN).
Ellie used to ...... (PRACTICE) for hours to learn to juggle properly.
That was the most ...... (INTEREST) book I've ever read.
Would you describe yourself as a ...... (COMPETE) person?
Many professional basketball players earn a ...... (FORTUNE) these days.
F.A. stands for Football ...... (ASSOCIATE).
The gold, silver and bronze ...... (MEDAL) took their places on the podium.
In Britain, the money parents give their children is often called pocket money. In America, it's often called an ...... (ALLOW).
I'm afraid the swimming pool is closed at the moment as they're carrying out some essential ...... (MAINTAIN).

I.title: Word formation (text)
I.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

questions.sentence: When I first started playing snooker, I had no real (1) ...... (KNOW) of the rules.
I just thought it looked an (2) ...... (ENJOY) game.
Also, I didn't need to buy any expensive (3) ...... (EQUIP) because the snooker hall near my house had tables and cues.
I spent (4) ...... (PRACTICAL) all the free time I had practising, and then decided to enter a (5) ...... (COMPETE).
My (6) ...... (OPPOSE) was someone who'd been playing for years.
Of course, he beat me, but the fact that I'd (7) ...... (LOSE) didn't put me off at all.
And, (8) ...... (FORTUNE), my game improved enough for me to become a professional snooker player three years ago.
